# cpp-t2 v2 Common-Mistakes Registry — INDEX

**Status:** Initial canonical CM database authored (CA-CM01 deliverable).
**CM count (this index):** 50 (Q1: 20, Q2: 8, Q3: 10, Q4: 4, cross-cutting: 8).
**Authoritative spec:** `docs/16_test2_specific_redesign_v2.md` §3.8, §4.6, §5.5, §6.5, §8.4.
**Schema:** YAML per file. Fields: `id`, `name`, `description`, `whyItHappens`, `immunizationStrategy`, `examImpact` (low/medium/high), `atomDependencies`, `qTracks`, `relatedCMs`, `source`.
**Generated by:** agent CA-CM01 (common-mistakes registry specialist).
**Coverage audit:** `coverage-audit.json` (initialised empty; populated by lint script post-card-authoring).

Per RULE 4 (`docs/17_option4_max_quality_plan.md`), the CM database is the immunization
backbone. Each card cross-references one or more `CM-*` ids via `commonMistakeIds`.

| Q1 | 22 | 11 |
| Q2 | 8 | 2 |

(Sums exceed 50 because some CMs span multiple Q-tracks.)

## Min-Card Allocation

Each CM mandates at least 6 immunization cards (per the strategies enumerated
in each YAML). That floor yields 50 × 6 = 300 CM-tagged cards minimum across
the deck. Cards typically tag 1-3 CMs simultaneously, so the actual unique
card count is lower (target: ~120-180 immunization cards within the 2,811 deck).

## Lint Rules (enforced by build script)

1. Every `commonMistakeIds: [...]` entry on a card must resolve to a file
   in this folder.
2. Every CM in this folder must be referenced by at least 6 cards
   (lint-fail otherwise).
3. Each `qTracks` entry must align with the cards' `qTags` (a CM tagged
   `qTracks: [Q1]` must be referenced by Q1 cards; cross-Q is fine).
4. `atomDependencies` must resolve to atom files under `data/v2/atoms/`.
5. `relatedCMs` must form a closed graph (no dangling refs).

(Lint script implementation pending — tracked in `coverage-audit.json`.)
